baseNote: All endpoints are mounted at the path you pass to app.use(). The examples below use /books as the prefix.

endpoints.description: Inserts one document. Auto-generates uuid and slug (from title_name). Runs Joi createSchema validation before insert. Reserved fields (uuid, slug, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) must not be sent.

body.note: If present, slug is auto-generated from it.
UUID of the owning user or entity.
Any additional fields declared in additionalFields.

endpoints.description: Accepts a top-level JSON array or { items: [] }. Validates every item before any insert. If one item fails validation the entire request is rejected with a message identifying the failing index.

body.note: Array of documents. Same shape as create-one body.

endpoints.summary: Paginated list of all non-deleted records.
endpoints.description: Returns paginated data with full sort, filter, and field projection support. Soft-deleted records are always excluded.

query.note: Filter by any field. Multiple filters are ANDed. e.g. filter[status]=active

endpoints.summary: Fetch a single record by uuid or MongoDB _id.
endpoints.description: Returns 404 if the record does not exist or has been soft-deleted. Accepts an optional fields projection.

endpoints.summary: Fetch a record with its registered child entities embedded as paginated sub-collections.
endpoints.description: Each child entity appears as a key in the response object whose value is a full PaginatedResult. Child pagination, sort, and depth are controlled via query params.

query.note: true = all registered children. Or comma-separated aliases e.g. chapters,reviews.
Page for a specific child set. Replace alias with the child's alias.
Page size for a specific child set.
Sort field for a specific child set.
Sort direction for a specific child set.

endpoints.description: Equivalent to a single-field filter on /all. Returns a full paginated result with all standard pagination params.

params.note: Any document field name. e.g. status, owned_by, libraryId.

endpoints.summary: Search records by a query string.
endpoints.description: Case-insensitive regex search when searchFields are provided. Falls back to MongoDB $text search (requires a text index) when no fields are given. Returns 400 if the q param is absent.

query.note: Comma-separated fields to search. Overrides entity's searchableFields for this request.

endpoints.summary: Return the total count of matching non-deleted records.
endpoints.description: Supports the same filter[field]=value params as /all. Soft-deleted records are excluded.

endpoints.summary: Check whether a record exists where :field equals :value.
endpoints.description: Always returns 200. Never 404. Useful for client-side uniqueness checks before a create.

params.note: Any document field. e.g. slug, isbn.

endpoints.summary: Update fields on an existing record.
endpoints.description: Only fields included in the body are changed. Fields not sent retain their current values. If title_name changes, slug is regenerated. Validation runs against updateSchema (all fields optional by default). Fires the update event and one update.fieldName event per changed key.

body.note: Any subset of the document's fields.

endpoints.summary: Update a single named field using dot-notation.
endpoints.description: Body must be { value: <newValue> }. Supports dot-notation for nested paths. Does not trigger Joi schema validation (the value is applied raw). Fires update events.

body.note: The new value to assign to the field.

endpoints.summary: Apply a single nested operation using a MongoDB operator.
endpoints.description: Surgical mutation of nested objects and arrays. Body must contain field (dot-path), operation (operator name), and value. The _mmid auto-injected on every array object item is used for patch_item and pull_id.

body.note: Dot-notation path to the target field.
One of: set, unset, push, push_many, pull, pull_id, patch_item, add_to_set, increment, rename.
Depends on the operation. See nested ops reference.

responses.label: Bad op
responses.example: { "message": "patch_item requires value._mmid to identify the target array element" }

endpoints.summary: Apply multiple nested operations in a single request.
endpoints.description: Accepts an array of operation objects or { ops: [] }. Non-patch_item operations are merged into a single updateOne call. patch_item operations each get their own call due to arrayFilters.

body.note: Array of { field, operation, value } objects.

endpoints.summary: Soft-delete a record by default. Pass ?hard=true for permanent removal.
endpoints.description: Soft delete sets deleted_at to now and status to archived. The record becomes invisible to all read endpoints but remains in the collection and can be restored. Hard delete (requires ?hard=true) permanently removes the document. The delete lifecycle event fires in both cases.

query.note: Pass hard=true to permanently remove the document.

endpoints.description: Sets deleted_at back to null and status back to active. The record becomes visible to all read endpoints again. The restore lifecycle event fires.
